subject: Chemistry

learning_objectives.statement: Represent a multistep process with an overall equilibrium expression, using the constituent K expressions for each individual reaction.
When a reaction is reversed, K is inverted.
When the stoichiometric coefficients of a reaction are multiplied by a factor c, K is raised to the power c.
When reactions are added together, the K of the resulting overall reaction is the product of the K's for the reactions that were summed.
Since the expressions for K and Q have identical mathematical forms, all valid algebraic manipulations of K also apply to Q.

depth_profile: Students are expected to understand and apply the mathematical properties of the equilibrium constant (K) and reaction quotient (Q) when manipulating chemical equations. This includes knowing that reversing a reaction inverts K, multiplying coefficients by a factor c raises K to the power of c, and adding reactions together requires multiplying their respective K values to find the overall K. Students must also recognize that these identical algebraic manipulations apply to Q.
assessment_notes: This topic is assessed alongside the suggested skill of Mathematical Routines (5.A), where students must identify quantities needed to solve a problem from given information (e.g., text, mathematical expressions, graphs, or tables). Students should be prepared to derive an overall equilibrium expression or calculate an overall K value from a series of constituent reactions.

spec_checklist.can_do: I can represent a multistep process with an overall equilibrium expression, using the constituent K expressions for each individual reaction.
I can invert the value of K when a chemical reaction is reversed.
I can raise K to the power of c when the stoichiometric coefficients of a reaction are multiplied by a factor c.
I can calculate the K of an overall reaction by multiplying the K values of the individual reactions that were summed together.
I can apply all valid algebraic manipulations of K to the reaction quotient Q.

spec_source_citation: AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description, Course Framework V.1 (2024 College Board)
